binaries.title: kdnic.sys (Kernel Debug Network Interface Card Driver) - NDIS timer/DPC -> NdisRegisterPoll + WDF refactor with ETW TraceLogging

changed_functions.name: RxReceiveIndicateDpc @ 0x1C0002460 (unpatched) -> HandleReceive @ 0x1400024F8 (patched)
key_changes: Receive handler moved from an NDIS timer callback (registered via NdisAllocateTimerObject, object at adapter+0x130) to an NdisPoll callback (HandleReceive), reached through NdisRegisterPoll

Unpatched has a 0x40-byte frame with no __security_cookie; patched has a 0xD0-byte frame with a cookie (loaded from cs:__security_cookie, stored at [rbp+0x17], checked at 0x1400027C2 via __security_check_cookie). The larger frame holds local ETW TraceLogging descriptor arrays, which is what the cookie protects

Both builds read the same fixed 6 bytes of the destination-MAC field from the driver-managed scatter/gather descriptor (unpatched [r8+0x14] dword + [r8+0x18] word into [rsp+0x68]; patched [r9+0x14] + [r9+0x18] into a stack slot) and pass a pointer to NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination. It is a fixed-size read into a fixed slot, not a variable-length copy

The doubly-linked-list back-pointer integrity fail-fast (cmp [rax+8],reg; jnz -> mov ecx,3; int 29h = RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY)) is present and identical in BOTH builds (unpatched target 0x1C0002658, patched target 0x14000271B). The patch does not add list integrity validation

Instead of indicating inline via NdisMIndicateReceiveNetBufferLists, the patched poll callback returns the built list to NDIS via its output parameter ([rsi+0x10] list, [rsi+0x18] count) and honours the per-poll budget (cmp r15d,[rsi]; jnb at 0x1400026CF)
Patched adds ETW TraceLogging via _tlgWriteTransfer_EtwWriteTransfer, gated by dword_140008000 > 5 (calls at 0x1400025A1 and 0x1400027BD)
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix. The added __security_cookie is a compiler artifact of the larger poll-model frame that now holds local ETW descriptor arrays, not protection for a newly found overflowable buffer. The unpatched handler has no overflowable stack buffer: the only stack write of descriptor data is a fixed 6-byte destination-MAC read into a fixed slot. The list-corruption fail-fast (int 29h, code 3) exists identically in both builds, and the poll-budget count limit is the NdisPoll API contract, not a bounds fix (the unpatched loop already drains a finite flushed SList).
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched prologue (RxReceiveIndicateDpc, NO stack cookie, 0x40 frame):
0x1C0002460  mov     [rsp+8], rbx
0x1C0002465  mov     [rsp+18h], rbp
0x1C000246A  push    rsi
0x1C000246B  push    rdi
0x1C000246C  push    r14
0x1C000246E  sub     rsp, 40h
0x1C0002472  mov     rdi, [rdx+30h]
0x1C0002476  mov     rbx, rdx

Patched prologue (HandleReceive, WITH stack cookie, 0xD0 frame):
0x1400024F8  mov     [rsp+8], rbx
0x1400024FD  push    rbp
0x1400024FE  push    rsi
0x1400024FF  push    rdi
0x140002500  push    r12
0x140002502  push    r13
0x140002504  push    r14
0x140002506  push    r15
0x140002508  lea     rbp, [rsp-27h]
0x14000250D  sub     rsp, 0D0h
0x140002514  mov     rax, cs:__security_cookie
0x14000251B  xor     rax, rsp
0x14000251E  mov     [rbp+17h], rax

Fixed 6-byte destination-MAC read (same in both; here unpatched):
0x1C0002515  lea     rcx, [rsp+68h]
0x1C000251A  mov     eax, [r8+14h]
0x1C000251E  mov     [rsp+68h], eax
0x1C0002522  movzx   eax, word ptr [r8+18h]
0x1C0002527  mov     [rsp+6Ch], ax
0x1C000252C  call    NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination

List-integrity fail-fast (same in both; here unpatched):
0x1C0002583  cmp     [rax+8], rdx
0x1C0002587  jnz     0x1C0002658
0x1C0002658  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C000265D  int     29h

Unpatched inline indicate (timer path):
0x1C000261F  mov     rcx, [rbx+28h]
0x1C0002623  xor     r8d, r8d
0x1C0002626  mov     rdx, rbp
0x1C0002629  mov     [rsp+20h], 401h
0x1C0002631  call    cs:__imp_NdisMIndicateReceiveNetBufferLists

Patched cookie check in epilogue:
0x1400027C2  mov     rcx, [rbp+17h]
0x1400027C6  xor     rcx, rsp
0x1400027C9  call    __security_check_cookie

changed_functions.name: TXSendCompleteDpc @ 0x1C00027D0 (unpatched) -> HandleSend @ 0x1400027F0 (patched)
key_changes: Transmit-completion handler moved from an NDIS timer callback (object at adapter+0xC0) to an NdisPoll callback (HandleSend)

Unpatched has a 0x30-byte frame (push rbx; sub rsp,0x30) with no __security_cookie; patched has a 0xA0-byte frame (sub rsp,0xA0) with a cookie at [rbp+0x17], checked before return. The cookie protects the new poll/ETW locals

Both flush the send-complete SList at adapter_ext+0x20 and, per completed transmit control block, either record a send failure (inc [rbx+0x2B0]; set NBL status 0xC000009A = STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES) or update send statistics (+0x248/+0x250/+0x258 counts, +0x278/+0x280/+0x288 byte totals). The status flag [rax+0x1C] and frame type [rdx+0x28] are fields the driver's own send path wrote, not attacker-supplied network data
Patched bounds the completion loop by the poll budget (cmp r12d,[r14]; jnb) and drains a new in-flight list at adapter+0x158
The list-corruption fail-fast (mov ecx,3; int 29h) is present in both builds (unpatched 0x1C0002902)
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix. Same reasoning as the receive handler: no overflowable stack buffer exists in the unpatched function, the completion metadata is driver-written (not attacker network data), the list-corruption fail-fast is present in both builds, and the added cookie / poll-budget limit follow from the poll-model rewrite and ETW instrumentation.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched prologue (TXSendCompleteDpc, NO cookie, 0x30 frame):
0x1C00027D0  push    rbx
0x1C00027D2  sub     rsp, 30h
0x1C00027D6  mov     rcx, [rdx+30h]
0x1C00027E6  add     rcx, 20h
0x1C00027EA  call    cs:__imp_ExpInterlockedFlushSList

Driver-written completion fields and failure branch (unpatched):
0x1C0002891  mov     eax, [rax+1Ch]
0x1C0002894  test    eax, eax
0x1C0002898  inc     dword ptr [rbx+2B0h]
0x1C00028A9  mov     dword ptr [rcx+8Ch], 0C000009Ah

List-corruption fail-fast (unpatched):
0x1C0002902  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C0002907  int     29h

Patched prologue (HandleSend, WITH cookie, 0xA0 frame):
0x1400027F0  mov     [rsp+18h], rbx
0x1400027F5  push    rbp
0x140002805  sub     rsp, 0A0h
0x14000280C  mov     rax, cs:__security_cookie
0x140002813  xor     rax, rsp
0x140002816  mov     [rbp+17h], rax
0x140002840  lea     rdi, [rbx+158h]

changed_functions.name: TXTransmitQueuedSends @ 0x1C0002920 (unpatched) -> 0x1400010C0 (patched)
key_changes: Pairs entries removed from two interlocked lists (unpatched adapter+0x60/+0x70 and adapter+0x80/+0x90 via ExInterlockedRemoveHeadList; offsets shift in patched) and pushes the resulting scatter/gather descriptors to an SList

Unpatched has a 0x30-byte frame with no __security_cookie; patched adds a cookie. There is no overflowable stack buffer and no attacker-controlled size in either build; the cookie tracks the larger patched frame / ETW locals
Patched adds ETW telemetry and restructures the loop
changed_functions.security_notes: Not a security fix. The function only unlinks and re-links driver-managed list entries and pushes descriptors; there is no stack buffer, no copy, and no attacker-controlled length. The added cookie is a compiler artifact of the refactor.

changed_functions.name: MPSendNetBufferLists @ 0x1C00021B0 (unpatched) -> 0x140002AD0 (patched)
key_changes: Field offsets shifted (0xd0->0xc0 for outstanding count, 0xc8->0xb8 for send queue count) due to adapter structure reorganization
Unpatched version inlines packet processing; patched version calls extracted TXQueueNetBufferForSend (0x140001290) for each NET_BUFFER
Error status calculation changed from explicit if-else to a computed expression

changed_functions.name: MPInitializeEx @ 0x1C00072C0 (unpatched) -> 0x14000B370 (patched)
key_changes: Patched version adds NdisRegisterPoll with two poll handlers (NdisSetPollNotification 0x140001690 and NdisPoll 0x1400017E0) at adapter+0x120
Patched version reads adapter parameters from different structure offsets due to the larger adapter structure (0x308 vs 0x2d0 bytes)
Error handling restructured: unpatched uses goto-based cleanup; patched uses linear flow with status tracking

changed_functions.name: NICAllocAdapter @ 0x1C000755C (unpatched) -> 0x14000B66C (patched)
key_changes: Allocation size changed from 0x2d0 to 0x308 bytes for the adapter context structure
Timer wiring reduced (timer-based polling partly replaced by NdisRegisterPoll)
List head and spinlock field offsets shifted throughout the structure

changed_functions.name: MPQueryInformation @ 0x1C0001B98 (unpatched) -> 0x140001EE0 (patched)
key_changes: Function rewritten to a table-driven OID dispatch approach; statistics field offsets shifted due to adapter structure reorganization

The unpatched build carries a __security_cookie; the patched build (0x140001EE0) drops it. Not a regression: the only stack buffer is the fixed vendor string "Microsoft" written with constant data and copied out to the caller, and every output copy is bounded by the same caller-length check in both builds (cmp ebx,[rsi+0x30]; ja -> error 0xC0010016, else memmove of size ebx). With no variable-length attacker write into the stack buffer, the stack-protection heuristic legitimately omits the cookie in the patched codegen

changed_functions.name: TCBFree @ 0x1C0002BB0 (unpatched) -> patched (TXNblReleaseAndComplete 0x140001A60 path)
key_changes: The DispatchLevel argument passed to the NBL-release/complete path changes from 1 (TRUE) to 0 (FALSE)
List head offset changed from 0x60/0x70 to 0x58/0x68
changed_functions.security_notes: The DispatchLevel flag change is an IRQL-claim correctness change on completion, not a memory-safety fix.

changed_functions.name: TXFlushSendQueue @ 0x1C0002668 (unpatched) -> 0x140002CB8 (patched)
key_changes: Unpatched drains lists at +0x80/+0x90 and +0xa0/+0xb0; patched drains shifted offsets plus the new in-flight list at +0x158/+0x168
Patched adds a drain loop for the new in-flight packet list with the same back-pointer int 29h fail-fast used elsewhere
Patched decrements count at +0x168 for each removed entry

changed_functions.name: MPSetInformation @ 0x1C0002010 (unpatched) -> 0x14000239C (patched)
key_changes: OID field offsets shifted due to adapter structure reorganization
Error handling restructured from early-return to linear flow
OID 0x1010e validation (mask check with 0xffffffd0) preserved and identical in both versions

changed_functions.name: DriverEntry @ 0x1C0009080 (unpatched) -> 0x14000D03C (patched)
key_changes: Patched version adds WDF class binding (WdfVersionBind, WdfLdrQueryInterface) alongside NdisMRegisterMiniportDriver
Patched version adds a DriverUnload hook
Patched version adds DbgPrintEx for failure diagnostics

security_findings.entry_point: Datapath receive handler. Unpatched: NDIS timer callback (NdisTimerObject at adapter+0x130). Patched: NdisPoll callback reached through NdisRegisterPoll.
call_chain: Receive SList at adapter_ext+0x40 drained via ExpInterlockedFlushSList
unpatched: NdisMIndicateReceiveNetBufferLists (inline indicate); patched: list returned to NDIS via poll output parameter
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (poll-model refactor; stack cookie added as a compiler artifact of new ETW-descriptor locals)
security_findings.description: The unpatched receive handler runs without a __security_cookie because it contains no overflowable stack buffer. The only descriptor data written to the stack is a fixed 6 bytes of the destination-MAC field, read from the driver-managed scatter/gather descriptor (unpatched: [r8+0x14] dword and [r8+0x18] word into [rsp+0x68]; patched: [r9+0x14]/[r9+0x18]) and passed by pointer to NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination. This is a fixed-size read into a fixed slot, not a variable-length copy, and cannot overflow. The doubly-linked-list back-pointer integrity fail-fast (int 29h, code 3 = RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY)) is present and identical in both builds. The patched HandleReceive gains a __security_cookie because its larger 0xD0-byte frame now holds local ETW TraceLogging descriptor arrays; the cookie protects those locals, not a newly discovered corruption bug. The behavioural change is architectural: the timer callback becomes an NdisPoll callback that returns the built list to NDIS through its output parameter and honours the poll budget (cmp r15d,[rsi]; jnb at 0x1400026CF) instead of indicating inline, plus added ETW tracing gated by dword_140008000 > 5.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. No attacker-controllable length or pointer reaches an unguarded write in either build. The receive path drains a finite flushed SList and classifies each frame by a fixed 6-byte MAC read, guarded in both builds by the same list-integrity fail-fast.
security_findings.primitive: None. No memory-corruption primitive exists in either build: no variable-length stack copy, no attacker-controlled size or pointer feeding an unchecked write, and identical list-integrity fail-fasts. The absence of a __security_cookie in the unpatched handler reflects that the compiler found no overflowable stack buffer to protect.
security_findings.evidence:
Unpatched RxReceiveIndicateDpc prologue (0x40 frame, NO cookie):
0x1C0002460  mov     [rsp+8], rbx
0x1C0002465  mov     [rsp+18h], rbp
0x1C000246A  push    rsi
0x1C000246B  push    rdi
0x1C000246C  push    r14
0x1C000246E  sub     rsp, 40h
0x1C0002472  mov     rdi, [rdx+30h]
0x1C0002476  mov     rbx, rdx

Fixed 6-byte destination-MAC read into a fixed stack slot:
0x1C0002515  lea     rcx, [rsp+68h]
0x1C000251A  mov     eax, [r8+14h]
0x1C000251E  mov     [rsp+68h], eax
0x1C0002522  movzx   eax, word ptr [r8+18h]
0x1C0002527  mov     [rsp+6Ch], ax
0x1C000252C  call    NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination

List-integrity fail-fast present in BOTH builds (here unpatched):
0x1C0002583  cmp     [rax+8], rdx
0x1C0002587  jnz     0x1C0002658
0x1C0002658  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C000265D  int     29h

Patched HandleReceive adds a cookie for its ETW-descriptor frame:
0x14000250D  sub     rsp, 0D0h
0x140002514  mov     rax, cs:__security_cookie
0x14000251B  xor     rax, rsp
0x14000251E  mov     [rbp+17h], rax
0x1400026CF  cmp     r15d, [rsi]        ; poll budget
0x1400026D2  jnb     0x140002722
0x14000271B  mov     ecx, 3             ; same fail-fast
0x140002720  int     29h
0x1400027C2  mov     rcx, [rbp+17h]
0x1400027C6  xor     rcx, rsp
0x1400027C9  call    __security_check_cookie
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched RxReceiveIndicateDpc @ 0x1C0002460 (full):
0x1C0002460  mov     [rsp+8], rbx
0x1C0002465  mov     [rsp+18h], rbp
0x1C000246A  push    rsi
0x1C000246B  push    rdi
0x1C000246C  push    r14
0x1C000246E  sub     rsp, 40h              ; 0x40 frame, no cookie slot
0x1C0002472  mov     rdi, [rdx+30h]        ; adapter extension
0x1C0002476  mov     rbx, rdx              ; adapter context
0x1C0002479  test    rdi, rdi
0x1C000247C  jz      0x1C000263D
0x1C0002489  mov     rcx, rbx
0x1C000248C  call    RCBAllocate
0x1C00024AB  call    cs:__imp_KdPollBreakIn
0x1C00024CC  lea     rcx, [rdi+40h]        ; received-packet SList head
0x1C00024D0  call    cs:__imp_ExpInterlockedFlushSList
0x1C00024DC  mov     rdx, rax
0x1C00024DF  test    rax, rax
0x1C00024E2  jz      0x1C000263D
0x1C00024E8  lea     rax, [rsp+30h]        ; stack-local list sentinel
0x1C00024ED  mov     [rsp+38h], rax
0x1C00024F7  mov     [rsp+30h], rax
0x1C00024FC  mov     rax, [rdx+20h]        ; NET_BUFFER_LIST
0x1C0002500  mov     r8, [rdx+28h]        ; scatter/gather descriptor
0x1C0002504  mov     r9, [rdx]            ; next entry
0x1C000250E  mov     eax, [r8+10h]        ; descriptor byte count
0x1C0002512  mov     [rcx+18h], eax
0x1C0002515  lea     rcx, [rsp+68h]
0x1C000251A  mov     eax, [r8+14h]        ; fixed 4-byte dest-MAC read
0x1C000251E  mov     [rsp+68h], eax
0x1C0002522  movzx   eax, word ptr [r8+18h] ; fixed 2-byte dest-MAC read
0x1C0002527  mov     [rsp+6Ch], ax
0x1C000252C  call    NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination
0x1C0002583  cmp     [rax+8], rdx         ; list back-pointer integrity check
0x1C0002587  jnz     0x1C0002658          ; -> RtlFailFast(3)
0x1C000261F  mov     rcx, [rbx+28h]       ; MiniportAdapterHandle
0x1C0002626  mov     rdx, rbp             ; NetBufferList chain
0x1C0002629  mov     [rsp+20h], 401h      ; ReceiveFlags (dispatch-level)
0x1C0002631  call    cs:__imp_NdisMIndicateReceiveNetBufferLists
0x1C000263D  mov     rcx, rbx
0x1C0002640  call    RXScheduleTheReceiveIndication
0x1C000264F  add     rsp, 40h
0x1C0002657  retn                          ; no cookie check
0x1C0002658  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C000265D  int     29h                   ; RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY)

breakpoints.why: Entry of the receive datapath handler. rdx = adapter context; [rdx+0x30] = adapter extension. Confirms the 0x40 frame and absence of a stack cookie (a compiler artifact of having no overflowable buffer, not a removed mitigation).

Call to NICGetFrameTypeFromDestination with rcx = [rsp+0x68]. The 6 bytes at [rsp+0x68] are the fixed destination-MAC field just read from the scatter/gather descriptor; observe that this is a fixed-size read, not a variable-length copy.

watch: rdx at entry = adapter context; [rdx+0x30] = adapter extension
[rdi+0x40] = received-packet SList head drained by ExpInterlockedFlushSList
[r8+0x14] (dword) and [r8+0x18] (word) = fixed destination-MAC field of the driver-managed scatter/gather descriptor, copied to the fixed slot [rsp+0x68]
[rsp+0x30] = stack-local list sentinel; back-pointer checked by the int 29h fail-fast present in both builds
rbx+0x230/0x238/0x240 and rbx+0x260/0x268/0x270 = per-frame-type packet and byte counters
key_offsets: 0x1C000246e: sub rsp, 0x40 - no cookie slot (no overflowable buffer to protect)
0x1C0002583-0x1C0002587: list back-pointer integrity check (identical in both builds)
0x1C0002658-0x1C000265d: RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY) landing pad (identical in both builds)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: kdnic.sys is the Kernel Debug Network Interface Card driver used for network kernel debugging (KDNET). In the unpatched build the timer callback at adapter+0x130 fires to drain the hardware receive SList; in the patched build the same work runs as an NdisPoll callback. No attacker-controllable length or pointer reaches an unguarded write in either build, so there is no corruption to set up.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: None. The receive path processes a fixed 6-byte descriptor field and a finite flushed SList, guarded in both builds by the same list-integrity fail-fast (int 29h, code 3). The only build difference visible at the prologue/epilogue is the poll-model stack cookie, which tracks the new ETW local storage.
debugging_aids.notes: Adapter context grows 0x2d0 -> 0x308 between builds; statistics and list-head offsets shift accordingly. The patched receive handler returns packet lists to the poll handler via its output parameter rather than calling NdisMIndicateReceiveNetBufferLists inline.

security_findings.entry_point: Transmit-completion handler. Unpatched: NDIS timer callback (NdisTimerObject at adapter+0xC0). Patched: NdisPoll callback.
call_chain: Send-complete SList at adapter_ext+0x20 drained via ExpInterlockedFlushSList
per completed transmit control block: record failure or update send statistics
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (send-completion timer callback -> NdisPoll callback; stack cookie added as compiler artifact)
security_findings.description: The unpatched transmit-completion handler runs without a __security_cookie (push rbx; sub rsp,0x30) and has no overflowable stack buffer. It flushes the send-complete SList at adapter_ext+0x20 and, per completed transmit control block, either records a send failure (inc [rbx+0x2B0]; sets NBL status 0xC000009A = STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES) or updates the send statistics (+0x248/+0x250/+0x258 counts, +0x278/+0x280/+0x288 byte totals). The completion flag it reads ([rax+0x1C]) and the frame type ([rdx+0x28]) are fields the driver's own send path wrote, not attacker-supplied network data. The list-corruption fail-fast (mov ecx,3; int 29h) is present in both builds. In the patched build HandleSend performs the same completion work as an NdisPoll callback, bounded by the poll budget (cmp r12d,[r14]; jnb) and using a new in-flight list at adapter+0x158; its 0xA0-byte frame carries a __security_cookie that tracks the new poll/ETW locals.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. The metadata driving statistics and completion status is written by the driver's own transmit path, not by network input, and there is no variable-length stack copy.
security_findings.primitive: None. No stack buffer, no attacker-controlled size, and the same list-corruption fail-fast in both builds.
security_findings.evidence:
Unpatched TXSendCompleteDpc prologue (0x30 frame, NO cookie):
0x1C00027D0  push    rbx
0x1C00027D2  sub     rsp, 30h
0x1C00027D6  mov     rcx, [rdx+30h]
0x1C00027E6  add     rcx, 20h              ; send-complete SList head
0x1C00027EA  call    cs:__imp_ExpInterlockedFlushSList

Driver-written completion fields, failure branch:
0x1C0002891  mov     eax, [rax+1Ch]        ; driver-set completion flag
0x1C0002894  test    eax, eax
0x1C0002898  inc     dword ptr [rbx+2B0h]  ; send-error count
0x1C00028A9  mov     dword ptr [rcx+8Ch], 0C000009Ah ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES

List-corruption fail-fast (present in both builds):
0x1C0002902  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C0002907  int     29h

Patched HandleSend adds a cookie for its 0xA0 poll/ETW frame:
0x140002805  sub     rsp, 0A0h
0x14000280C  mov     rax, cs:__security_cookie
0x140002813  xor     rax, rsp
0x140002816  mov     [rbp+17h], rax
0x140002840  lea     rdi, [rbx+158h]       ; new in-flight list
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched TXSendCompleteDpc @ 0x1C00027D0 (key path):
0x1C00027D0  push    rbx
0x1C00027D2  sub     rsp, 30h              ; 0x30 frame, no cookie, no stack buffer
0x1C00027D6  mov     rcx, [rdx+30h]        ; adapter extension
0x1C00027E6  add     rcx, 20h
0x1C00027EA  call    cs:__imp_ExpInterlockedFlushSList ; send-complete SList
0x1C0002816  mov     rdx, [rcx]
0x1C000282E  cmp     [rcx+8], r8          ; list integrity check
0x1C0002832  jnz     0x1C0002902          ; -> RtlFailFast(3)
0x1C0002891  mov     eax, [rax+1Ch]       ; driver-set completion flag
0x1C0002894  test    eax, eax
0x1C0002896  jnz     0x1C00028B5
0x1C0002898  inc     dword ptr [rbx+2B0h] ; send-error count
0x1C00028A9  mov     dword ptr [rcx+8Ch], 0C000009Ah ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES
0x1C00028B5  mov     ecx, [rdx+28h]       ; frame type (driver-written)
0x1C00028C2  inc     qword ptr [rbx+248h] ; tx unicast count
0x1C00028C9  add     [rbx+278h], rax      ; tx unicast bytes
0x1C00028F3  call    TCBFree
0x1C0002902  mov     ecx, 3
0x1C0002907  int     29h                  ; RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY)
0x1C000290C  call    TXTransmitQueuedSends
0x1C0002916  retn

breakpoints.why: Entry of the transmit-completion handler. Confirms the 0x30 frame and no stack cookie; rdx = adapter context, [rdx+0x30] = adapter extension whose +0x20 holds the send-complete SList.

watch: [rdx+0x30] = adapter extension; ext+0x20 = send-complete SList head
[rdx+0x28] = frame type (driver-written), selects the statistics bucket
rbx+0x248/0x250/0x258 and rbx+0x278/0x280/0x288 = tx count/byte statistics
key_offsets: 0x1C00027d2: sub rsp, 0x30 - no cookie, no stack buffer
0x1C0002902-0x1C0002907: RtlFailFast landing pad (present in both builds)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Fires when NDIS completes previously submitted transmits and the driver queues them to the send-complete SList at adapter_ext+0x20. In the patched build the same completion work runs as an NdisPoll callback. No attacker-controlled input reaches a memory-unsafe operation.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: None. Completion metadata is driver-written; the same list-corruption fail-fast guards both builds.
debugging_aids.notes: The patched HandleSend uses a 0xA0-byte frame with a cookie at [rbp+0x17] and a poll-budget limit (cmp r12d,[r14]; jnb), plus a new in-flight list at adapter+0x158 - all consequences of the poll-model rewrite, not a corruption fix.

summary: The patch migrates kdnic.sys (Windows Kernel Debug Network Interface Card driver) from the legacy NDIS timer-callback/DPC datapath to the newer NdisRegisterPoll poll model, adds WDF class binding (WdfVersionBind, WdfLdrQueryInterface) and a DriverUnload hook, and adds ETW TraceLogging instrumentation. 43 of 58 matched functions changed and overall similarity is ~49%, consistent with a broad architectural rewrite rather than a targeted security fix. The receive and send-completion handlers were renamed and rebuilt as poll callbacks (RxReceiveIndicateDpc -> HandleReceive, TXSendCompleteDpc -> HandleSend). The __security_cookie that appears in the rewritten handlers is a compiler artifact of their new, larger stack frames, which now hold local ETW TraceLogging descriptor arrays; the unpatched handlers lacked a cookie because they contain no overflowable stack buffer (their only descriptor-data stack write is a fixed 6-byte destination-MAC read into a fixed slot). The doubly-linked-list integrity fail-fasts (RtlFailFast(FAST_FAIL_CORRUPT_LIST_ENTRY), int 29h with code 3) are present and identical in both builds, and the poll-budget count limits are the NdisPoll API contract rather than a bounds fix. The classic input-validation sites are unchanged: NICSetMulticastList keeps its multiple-of-6 check (error 0xC0010014) and 0xC0 cap (error 0xC0010009) with a bounded memset/memmove, MPSetInformation keeps its OID 0x1010E mask check, and NICReadRegParameters only reads admin-controlled NDIS configuration values. No security-relevant behavioral difference was found and no exploitable primitive exists in either build.
